impression: Patient with Child Class C cirrhosis due to alcohol and untreated hepatitis C (genotype 3), complicated by esophageal varices, ascites, and hepatic encephalopathy, presenting with acute on chronic right upper quadrant abdominal pain, vomiting, and diarrhea likely due to viral gastroenteritis. Imaging shows new partially occlusive portal vein thrombosis, large volume ascites, worsening splenomegaly, and a new 1.6 cm hepatic lesion requiring follow-up MRI. Hospital course complicated by hyponatremia and non-occlusive portal vein thrombosis; anticoagulation was deferred due to compliance concerns. Patient treated with IV fluids, pain control, antiemetics, and heparin drip. Discharged home with stable condition and instructions for follow-up.
indication: Right upper quadrant abdominal pain, vomiting, and diarrhea in a patient with cirrhosis and history of multiple abdominal surgeries.

Tiny 6 mm pseudoaneurysm in left superficial femoral artery distal to origin, superior to prior active extravasation site. Left superficial femoral arterial catheter in place.
indication: Evaluation for source of infection including left groin and bowel ischemia in a cirrhotic patient with bacteremia, UTI, rising leukocytosis, and left femoral line.

impression: Patient with Child C NASH cirrhosis complicated by diuretic refractory ascites, severe malnutrition, atrial fibrillation, type 2 diabetes, coronary artery disease, hyperthyroidism, and renal insufficiency. Presented with volume overload, fatigue, and weakness. Underwent multiple paracenteses, cardiac catheterization, hemodialysis line placement, central venous line placement, and arterial line placement. Developed hepatorenal syndrome requiring midodrine, octreotide, albumin, and dialysis. Experienced septic shock with infections including Serratia UTI, E. coli bacteremia, and MSSA ventilator-associated pneumonia, treated with antibiotics and antifungals. Persistent leukocytosis without clear infection source. Respiratory failure with prolonged intubation, thick secretions, and left lower lobe collapse. Patient declined tracheostomy and opted for comfort care, passed away shortly after extubation.

impression: Patient with advanced Alzheimer's dementia, chronic hepatitis C, autoimmune hepatitis, and atrial fibrillation presenting with right lower extremity deep vein thrombosis. Due to high bleeding risk from an intracranial meningioma and falls, anticoagulation was deferred and a permanent infrarenal inferior vena cava filter was successfully placed. Patient was treated with antibiotics for asymptomatic bacteriuria and continued on seizure prophylaxis with Keppra. Atrial fibrillation was rate controlled. Patient discharged to extended care with stable condition and clear, alert mental status.
indication: Right lower extremity deep vein thrombosis in a patient with Alzheimer's dementia, high bleeding risk due to meningioma and falls, requiring IVC filter placement.

Underwent right toe amputation and additional debridement. Initiated on hemodialysis for worsening ESRD. Treated with IV vancomycin, ceftazidime, and oral metronidazole. Discharged to rehabilitation with clear and coherent mental status, alert and interactive, ambulatory with assistance. Medications include insulin glargine, lisinopril, calcitriol, epoetin, atorvastatin, ferrous sulfate, vitamin D, and others. Follow-up with podiatry and infectious disease recommended.
indication: Nausea and vomiting in a patient with poorly controlled diabetes, hypertension, stage 5 CKD, neuropathy, and chronic right foot wound.
